title: Zehneria thwaitesii
taxonomy: Zehneria thwaitesii is classified within the family Cucurbitaceae, commonly known as the gourd or cucumber family. This family comprises a wide variety of plants, including cucumbers, melons, and gourds. The genus Zehneria is a group of annual or perennial vines native to various parts of the world. The species Zehneria thwaitesii is specifically found in Sri Lanka and is considered an endemic species. Further classification of Zehneria thwaitesii includes its placement in the tribe Zehnerieae within the family Cucurbitaceae.
uses: Zehneria thwaitesii is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Sri Lanka. The plant's leaves and roots are utilized to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its medicinal properties also help in reducing inflammation and are used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Furthermore, it is also used to treat gastrointestinal problems such as diarrhea and stomach pain. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.

intro: Zehneria thwaitesii, also known as Thwaites' cordia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cucurbitaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Sri Lankan medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Zehneria thwaitesii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments, as well as dried powder for oral consumption.
